100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 3, 1926, the British General Strike entered its third day, as workers across the UK halted production in solidarity with coal miners, sparking widespread unrest and economic disruption. In the United States, the Supreme Court ruled in favor of the right to picket, a significant win for labor unions amid ongoing labor disputes. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated as the Weimar Republic faced challenges from both leftist and rightist factions, raising concerns about stability in the region.
Updated: Sunday, May 3, 2026 at 07:36:42 AM CDT
